mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and falling consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand recovery, generating positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative median 60-day returns over a 5-year backtest, or interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a tightening cycle confirms resilient supply-side growth that offsets higher rates, producing positive weekly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of tightening periods across historical data.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across all macro regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above threshold consistently precede positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market expansion, driving positive monthly S&P 500 returns through earnings optimism.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields negative median returns in more than 50% of observations over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during a late-cycle expansion signal weakening consumer confidence and reduced construction activity, causing negative weekly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: HOUST declines in late-cycle periods are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of cases over historical data.
